figures.caption: Figure 2. Comparison of existing approaches and our method. (a) Prompt learning and text-free learning approaches. (b) Our proposed CG-CLIP framework that leverages text captions.
Table 2. Comparison with state-of-the-art methods across datasets. The bold and underline denote the best and second results, respectively.
Figure 6. Visualization of attention maps on SportsVReID. (a) Original images, (b) Vanilla CLIP, (c) TF-CLIP, (d) CG-CLIP. Our method effectively focuses on fine-grained, discriminative regions.

Figure 3. Illustration of our proposed CG-CLIP framework. Caption-guided Memory Refinement (CMR) refines Image Memory based on synthesized captions to capture fine-grained identity-specific features. Token-based Feature Extraction (TFE) aggregates features along both temporal and spatial dimensions using a cross-attention mechanism with learnable tokens.
